programmes.show_description: Bad girls don't come to London, they're made in London. Suzy, a young country girl, is led astray by the wild members of a rock group. Contains nudity and 70s social attitudes.
1/3. Alan Titchmarsh and the team help a 15-year-old boy confined to his wheelchair who has become increasingly confined to his house and cannot access the garden.
2/5. The castle marks the release of the second Downton Abbey film with an interview on the Lorraine breakfast show and a trip to London's Leicester Square for the premiere
The social history of Kings Cross rail workers with stories from coal-shovelling firemen on 1950s steam trains to Wilston Jackson, the UK's first Black train driver.

2/3. Alan meets Anne and Mike who has dementia. The layout of the house and garden means Mike is finding it difficult to cope and risks never leaving his home.
3/15. Rick meets inventor James Dyson on his futuristic Lincolnshire farm, where robots grow strawberries all year round. With superb local sausages, he makes bangers and mash.
3/15. Footballer Jill Scott joins Robson on his great outdoor adventure in North Yorkshire. They attempt paddleboard yoga and visit a walled garden.
Tonia Buxton visits the island of Crete where she creates Cretan pork, cooked with aubergines, bay leaves and cumin seeds. After that, halvas is the pudding of the day with its semolina-base.(S1/E3)
Scottish-born actor Alan Cumming explores the picturesque Outer Hebrides of Scotland, touring the Western Isles from Barra to Stornoway on Lewis, Eriskay and the Callanish stones.
5/8. In Kent, metal worker John tackles a cut-price riverboat restoration using ingenious workarounds. Can he make a home of his own?

programmes.show_name: Mary Millington: Come Play With Me
programmes.show_description: Two hapless forgers hide out in a Scottish manor house. When a troupe of dancing girls join them musical numbers, comedy and sauce ensues. Contains sexual scenes.
A playboy astrologer has to prove his alibi for a years-old robbery. With Mary Millington and Diana Dors. Contains sexual scenes and 70s social attitudes.

Cinema projectionists make magic with light. As digital projection makes them obsolete, this film records the working lives of projectionists in the heyday of this dying art.

Interviews with 19 men and women who were part of the Battle of Britain - unique accounts of the parts they played in the nation's defence, in their own words.
Alistair MacLean thriller. An American agent arrives in Amsterdam to investigate a drug- smuggling ring. He takes matters into his own hands. Classic speedboat-chase action.

Raucous period romp following a poor country girl who goes to London in pursuit of wealth, happiness and a bit of the other. Stars Ollie Reed and Lisa Forster. Contains sex scenes.
